details: Investigating fp32 S=8192 rms near-miss (0.0284 vs 0.02). Snapshot committed.
S=2048 rms=0.0087, S=8192 rms=0.0284 (HiFi4 default). Scale=0.125 and reduce scalers 1.0 are exact in bf16 (not error sources). Building TF32 simulation to isolate.

Simulation: TF32 matmul truncation gives only rms=0.0004 (flat in S), NOT 0.028. tf32+tf32-acc=0.003. exp relative err ~0.4pct reproduces 0.028. Root cause is likely SFPU exp precision x num_kv_blocks, NOT TF32 matmul accumulation. Testing on device.

Device rms grows ~sqrt(num_kv_blocks) x mild systematic: 1024->0.0059, 2048->0.0087, 4096->0.0151, 8192->0.0284. math_approx_mode=True worse everywhere (confirms exp precision matters, accurate exp already default). Per-block recurrence rounding accumulation confirmed; TF32 matmul ruled out by sim (flat 0.0004).

Root cause: SFPU exp precision accumulated over 256-block online-softmax recurrence. TF32 matmul falsified by sim (flat 0.0004). Kernel already at max precision (HiFi4+fp32_dest_acc+accurate exp+fp32 CBs). No descriptor/helper lever closes it. Confidence HIGH.
committed initial state before investigation; symptom bf8b fp16-DEST PCC=0.0475

work unit u=0 (qi=0, single KV block): bf8b QK post-scale and final output BYTE-IDENTICAL to bf16 and correct. QK matmul is NOT the bug. Corruption must arise in multi-KV-block work units (online accumulation).

EARLIER correct results were bf16 (probe argv bug: stdin scripts get no argv, so DT defaulted to bf16). With true bf8b: QK^T matmul output is GARBAGE — row0 = -7.7e-37,-3.2e-36,... instead of expected 1.34,0.89,2.06. Diagonal post-mask -6.6e-38 vs ~1.34. Corruption IS at the QK matmul reading bf8b into fp16 DEST.
